data.title: William Kim appointed as Chief Lifestyle Officer for Project B, focusing on premium lifestyle business as the global grand prix expands across Europe, Asia, Americas.
data.description: Project B, a leading global women's and men's basketball grand prix catering to a modern audience, has appointed William Kim as its Chief Lifestyle Officer. Kim will oversee Project B's luxury lifestyle division, focusing on merchandise, brand partnerships, and cultural collaborations as the organization grows across Europe, Asia, and the Americas. The aim is to establish Project B as the top cultural platform in global sports.

data.title: Willow and HerMoney Media partner to offer financial planning for women to grow wealth and confidence.
data.description: In Boston, Willow (TrustWillow.com), a renowned WealthTech platform connecting women and families with tailored financial guidance, has revealed a key alliance with HerMoney Media. The partnership involves joining forces with the digital platform dedicated to offering personal finance content and coaching to empower women in financial matters. Both platforms share a common goal of enhancing financial confidence and wealth growth for women. The collaboration aims to further support this mission and expand outreach to more women seeking financial empowerment.

data.title: Costs of senior care rising: Assisted living now $5,419/month, memory care $6,690/month, independent living $3,200/month.
data.description: In New York, A Place for Mom, a significant platform aiding families through aging stages, unveiled its 2026 Costs of Long-Term Care and Senior Living Report, offering a detailed look at senior care pricing in the US. The report shows a rise in monthly costs, with national median costs exceeding $5,419 for assisted living (up from $5,190 in 2025) and $6,690 for memory care (up from $6,450). Independent living averages at $3,200.

data.title: Dividend 15 Split Corp declares $0.10 monthly for Class A ($1.20/yr) & $0.05833 for Preferred ($0.70/yr) on Feb. 19.
data.description: In a recent declaration, Dividend 15 Split Corp. announced its monthly distribution for shareholders. The company will be paying out $0.10000 for each Class A share, which amounts to $1.20 annually. Additionally, $0.05833 will be distributed for each Preferred share, totaling $0.700 per share annually. Shareholders can expect these distributions to be paid on March 10, 2026. To qualify for the upcoming distribution, shareholders must be recorded as shareholders by February 27, 2026.

data.title: February US automotive sales rise compared to January per JD Power global forecast; lower EV demand impact remains.
data.description: In Troy, Michigan, according to JD Power, February saw an improvement in sales compared to January. However, the impact of reduced demand for electric vehicles continues to be evident. Key numbers indicate a notable increase in sales figures for the month, though concerns persist regarding the lingering effects of decreased interest in EVs on the overall market.

data.title: Celebrate 12th Annual Teach Music Week with FREE lessons at 1,200+ locations. Happening March 16-22, 2026.
data.description: Philadelphia, on February 19, 2026 - Keep Music Alive is pleased to reveal the upcoming 12th Annual Teach Music Week set to take place from Monday, March 16th to Sunday, March 22nd, 2026. This highly anticipated week-long event serves as a celebration that welcomes participation from musicians, music schools, music stores, and various music-related entities.

data.title: Goody® and Wet Brush® join Mackenzie-Childs for exclusive hair accessories collection; launching in Feb 2026.
data.description: Two prominent hair brands, Goody® and Wet Brush®, have joined forces with a popular lifestyle brand to introduce unique designs and vibrant colors to their range of daily hair products. The collaboration, unveiled in New York on Feb. 19, 2026, aims to infuse a fresh aesthetic into their essential hair tools. Goody® and Wet Brush® are renowned leaders in the hair accessories industry.

data.description: Los Cabos continues to focus on quality rather than quantity in its tourism approach, emphasizing high-value experiential travel with a strong emphasis on wellness, sustainability, luxury, and personalized experiences. data.title: American Signature home furnishings chain faces liquidation, with 89 stores and warehouses across 16 states up for sale.
data.description: A real estate firm, A&G Real Estate Partners, has been commissioned to oversee the sale of 89 stores and warehouses located in 16 states across the United States. This initiative comes as part of the liquidation process for the home furnishings retailer American Signature, with A&G representing the successful stalking-horse bidder. The extensive portfolio being marketed includes a range of commercial properties, indicating a substantial transaction volume that will impact the national real estate market.

data.title: Tom Madigan joins Thimble Island Private Wealth, bringing $300 million in assets from UBS to LPL Financial.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, LPL Financial LLC revealed that financial advisor Tom Madigan, CEPA®, has recently partnered with the company's broker-dealer, Registered Investment Advisor (RIA), and custodial platforms. He will be collaborating with Thimble Island Private Wealth, a firm already backed by LPL Strategic Wealth (SW). Madigan brings with him a substantial $300 million in advisory, brokerage, and retirement assets* and has made this transition from UBS.

data.title: ModelFront introduces Automatic Post-Editing (APE), now available to all customers, starting in 2024. Quality Prediction (QP) AI improves translation quality.
data.description: In Palo Alto, California, ModelFront has unveiled the launch of automatic post-editing (APE), an exclusive custom large language model. Initially introduced in 2024, APE is now accessible to all ModelFront clients, being automatically integrated into production and included as a standard feature from the beginning. ModelFront's quality prediction (QP) uses AI to evaluate and amend AI translations. It autonomously assesses segments that would typically require human verification in their unedited state, prompting human involvement only for those that necessitate further attention.

data.title: Amplitude Inc (NASDAQ: AMPL) reported Q4 earnings in line with estimates, but forecasted lower adjusted EPS and sales for FY2026.
data.description: Amplitude Inc announced its fourth-quarter earnings, with reported earnings of 4 cents per share, in line with analyst expectations. The company's quarterly sales totaled $91.427 million, surpassing the estimated $90.344 million. Looking ahead to FY2026, Amplitude anticipates an adjusted EPS of between 8 cents and 13 cents, falling short of the market's 12-cent estimate. Sales projections for the year range from $390.000 million to $398.000 million, slightly below estimates of $390.120 million. The company noted a shift towards AI-driven analytics, emphasizing enhanced product monitoring capabilities.
